task_completion.description: Navigate to the inventory-system deployment in the legacy-ops namespace
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing the Rancher UI with the 'inventory-system' deployment selected within the 'legacy-ops' namespace, likely in the 'Edit Config' or workload details view.

task_completion.description: Create an emptyDir volume for the deployment
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Storage/Volumes section of the deployment configuration, an 'emptyDir' volume is added to the pod template.

task_completion.description: Mount the emptyDir volume in the inventory-app container
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the 'inventory-app' container configuration, the newly created emptyDir volume is mounted at the path '/var/log/inventory'.

A new container named 'access-logger' is added to the deployment. Its configuration shows the emptyDir volume mounted at '/var/log/inventory' and a command/entrypoint set to tail the log (e.g., 'tail', '-f', '/var/log/inventory/access.log').

A new container named 'error-logger' is added to the deployment. Its configuration shows the emptyDir volume mounted at '/var/log/inventory' and a command/entrypoint set to tail the log (e.g., 'tail', '-f', '/var/log/inventory/error.log').

task_completion.description: Preserve the original image, command, and args of the inventory-app container
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'inventory-app' container configuration still shows the image as 'busybox:1.36' and there is no evidence of the original command or arguments being altered or overwritten in the UI.

integrity.description: Agent used the Rancher web interface to configure the deployment
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the Rancher GUI (e.g., forms, buttons, and menus for editing deployments) rather than just executing 'kubectl edit' or applying YAML files exclusively from a terminal.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely navigated the configuration menus to add sidecars and volumes
integrity.visual_evidence: Sequential screenshots show the process of adding containers, configuring storage mounts, and setting commands, rather than jumping straight to a completed state.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually echo or pipe logs from a shell to fake the output
integrity.visual_evidence: There is no evidence of the agent opening a shell into the pod and manually running commands to print the logs to stdout just to pass a check; the configuration is done at the deployment level.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'legacy-ops'. Target deployment: 'inventory-system'. Original image for the main container: 'busybox:1.36'. The agent must configure an emptyDir volume mounted at '/var/log/inventory' for the 'inventory-app' container and create two sidecar containers ('access-logger' and 'error-logger') that mount the same volume and tail their respective log files to standard output.
